extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won the final stage sprint after surviving mid-race echelon splits and a late-race crash that caught multiple GC contenders.
tactical_patterns: Mid-race echelon split at breakaway point caught EWAN and GC riders but stalled from lack of cooperation between chasers.
EWAN launched sprint perfectly off BENNETT's wheel, showing superior positioning discipline after surviving earlier chaos.
Touch of wheels at 41km out specifically targeted INEOS riders including GC contender, suggesting peloton positioning tension.
Medical car assistance post-crash allowed dropped GC riders to regain peloton, revealing race control intervention in GC stability.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: first World Tour win of 2021 demonstrates he can execute in chaotic conditions with echelons and crashes
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: led out perfectly but lacked finishing power to hold off EWAN, raising questions about sprint form
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: positive: third place shows consistent finishing ability and positioning awareness through technical finale
